summary.intent: Address Codex review feedback on PR #682 in fogodev/ars-ui, specifically fixing a controlled progress completion state bug and resolving review threads related to ARIA attributes and localization.
summary.outcome: Fixed controlled `Complete` state in the progress component to preserve the controlled value instead of forcing `max`, committed the patch, resolved review threads with rationale, and re-triggered Codex review. All local tests (unit, integration, proptest, fmt, clippy) passed.

repo: The agnostic-core contract for progress/meter requires root_attrs() to emit role='meter', aria-valuemin, and aria-valuemax — adapter-level filtering is out of scope for the core PR
Determinate progress value text must go through Messages::determinate to remain localizable
PR #682 uses Codex bot (@codex review trigger) for automated review cycles, with 👀 reaction indicating in-progress review
The project uses cargo features: `--features i18n` is required for the relevant test suites
There is a proptest for state machines under `--test proptest_state_machines data_display` that requires `--ignored` flag to run

code.finding: Complete state transition was incorrectly forcing max instead of computing target from the effective controlled value; also failed to rebind controlled context value to prop value on Complete

Existing regression test `progress_complete_event_sets_public_completion_consistently` was asserting the wrong (buggy) behavior — it was updated to encode the correct controlled-source-of-truth contract

workflow: Polling GitHub API (reactions + review threads + CI checks) in sleep loops is the pattern used to monitor Codex bot progress without webhooks
Review thread replies citing the issue plan / task contract are used to justify 'not changing' decisions on Codex P2 findings
Commit trailers include `Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>` when fixing Codex-flagged issues
The fix sequence is: regression test first → minimal patch → focused test → broader suite → fmt/clippy → commit

friction: Long polling loops with sleep intervals (120s, 180s, 240s) were needed to wait for Codex reactions and CI results, making the session slow
Unit Tests CI job was consistently the last pending check, creating extended wait times before knowing final CI state
Multiple context resumptions mid-session required re-verifying live PR state before acting, adding overhead
open_items: Native-host adapter filtering of ARIA attributes (aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax, role=meter) is explicitly deferred to a future adapter-boundary PR
Awaiting Codex's final reaction/verdict on the latest push after the controlled Complete fix
Unit Tests CI job result was still pending at end of session — final green/red status unknown
